phrases.intent_en: Ask for a boutique selling local Québec crafts
phrases.text_local: Quelle boutique vend des produits artisanaux du Québec ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel boo-TEEK vahn day pro-DWEE ar-tee-zah-NOH doo kay-BEK
phrases.cultural_note: Rue du Petit-Champlain is one of North America's oldest commercial streets. Many shops sell handcrafted local pottery, blown glass, and jewellery.

responses.english: There are several artisan shops on the street.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ah ploo-ZYUR boo-TEEK ar-tee-zah-NAHL soor lah ROO
responses.french: Il y a plusieurs boutiques artisanales sur la rue.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the mural on the cliff wall
phrases.text_local: C'est quoi la fresque sur la falaise ?
phrases.pronunciation: say KWAH lah FRESK soor lah fah-LEZ
phrases.cultural_note: The Fresque des Québécois is a massive trompe-l'oeil mural depicting key figures in Québec City history on a building wall near Place Royale.

responses.english: It is the Quebecers' mural, right there.
responses.phonetic: say lah FRESK day kay-bay-KWAH zhoost LAH
responses.french: C'est la fresque des Québécois, juste là.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for directions to the funicular
phrases.text_local: Le funiculaire pour monter en Haute-Ville, c'est par où ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh foo-nee-kyoo-LAIR poor mon-TAY ahn oht-VEEL say par OO
phrases.cultural_note: The funicular connects Basse-Ville to Haute-Ville and has operated since 1879. It's a short ride up the cliff face and a Québec City icon.

responses.english: The funicular is at the end of the street.
responses.phonetic: luh foo-nee-koo-LAIR eh toh BOO duh lah ROO
responses.french: Le funiculaire est au bout de la rue.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a maple taffy on snow experience
phrases.text_local: Y'a-tu un endroit pour faire de la tire d'érable sur neige par ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: yah-TOO un on-DWAH poor FAIR duh lah TEER day-RAHBL soor NAYJ par ee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: Tire sur neige (maple taffy on snow) is a Québec winter tradition where hot maple syrup is poured on snow and rolled up on a stick.

responses.phonetic: lay kah-BAHN ah SOO-kruh OOV-ruh ahn MARS
responses.french: Les cabanes à sucre ouvrent en mars.

phrases.intent_en: Ask a shopkeeper to wrap a gift
phrases.text_local: Pouvez-vous faire un emballage cadeau, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-VAY voo FAIR un ahm-bah-LAHZH kah-DOH seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: Most boutiques in Petit-Champlain offer gift wrapping, especially around Christmas when the neighbourhood becomes a magical winter scene.

responses.english: Yes, I will wrap it for you.
